data.content:
# Implementation plan — foolery-e55a (Approval escalation API)

## Goal recap

Promote approval escalations from a per-`SessionEntry` Map (today only
exposed via `GET /api/terminal[].pendingApprovals`) to a first-class,
session-independent backend resource:

- `GET /api/approvals` — list canonical approvals, with filters
- `POST /api/approvals/{approvalId}/actions` — approve / always_approve / reject
- Records survive `TerminalSession` cleanup; `actionable` becomes false
  rather than disappearing.
- OpenAPI fully documents the new surface plus the existing
  session-scoped compat route.

## Architecture (one design choice that matters)

Today `recordPendingApproval` writes into `entry.pendingApprovals` (Map
on `SessionEntry`). When `sessions.delete(id)` runs after exit/abort/cleanup
(see `terminal-manager.ts:464`, `terminal-manager-initial-finish.ts:81`,
`terminal-manager-initial-child-helpers.ts:374`, `orchestration-session-lifecycle.ts:88`,
`local-worker.ts:73,77`, `terminal-test-fixture.ts:81`), the records vanish.

Plan: introduce a **session-independent canonical registry** that stores the
*same `PendingApprovalRecord` references* the entry already holds. Both data
structures point at one record object, so existing entry-side reads (e.g.
`listSessions()` in `terminal-manager.ts:99`) stay correct. When the session
is removed we don't delete from the registry — instead we mark the responder
as detached, and `actionable` becomes false on listing.

This keeps the diff small (no rewiring of `recordPendingApproval`'s callers,
no double-source-of-truth for status/updates) while making the registry
durable past session lifetime.

## Step 1 — `approval-registry` module

**New: `src/lib/approval-registry.ts`**

Singleton stored on `globalThis` (mirrors `terminal-session-registry.ts`):

```
interface ApprovalRegistryEntry {
  record: PendingApprovalRecord;       // shared ref with entry.pendingApprovals
  responder:
    | ((record, action) => Promise<ApprovalReplyResult>)
    | null;                             // nulled on session detach
  agentInfo: ExecutionAgentInfo | undefined;  // snapshotted at register time
}
```

API:
- `registerApproval(sessionId, record, responder, agentInfo): void`
  Idempotent on `record.approvalId`; refreshes responder/agentInfo.
- `attachResponder(sessionId, responder)` — for late-bound takes.
- `detachSession(sessionId, reason): void` — sets responder=null on every
  registry entry whose `record.terminalSessionId === sessionId`.
- `getApproval(approvalId): ApprovalRegistryEntry | undefined`
- `listApprovals(filter): ApprovalEscalationDto[]`
  - filters: `repoPath`, `status` (single/array), `activeOnly`, `updatedSince`
  - deterministic ordering: `updatedAt desc`, tie-break `approvalId asc`
- `applyApprovalAction(approvalId, action): ApprovalActionResult`
  - 404 if unknown id
  - 409 with reason `approval_responder_unavailable` (records the failure
    state but does NOT remove the entry) if `responder == null`
  - delegates to existing `performApprovalAction` semantics otherwise

`ApprovalEscalationDto` is the public list/get shape — mirrors
`PendingApprovalRecord` plus computed:
- `actionable: boolean` — `responder!=null && supportedActions.length>0 && replyTarget!=null && isActiveApprovalStatus(status)`
- `actionableReason: string | undefined` — when not actionable, the reason
- `agent: { provider, name, model, version } | undefined` — snapshotted

## Step 2 — wire `terminal-approval-session.ts` to the registry

`src/lib/terminal-approval-session.ts`:

- `recordPendingApproval(entry, request)` — after building the record and
  storing it in `entry.pendingApprovals`, call
  `approvalRegistry.registerApproval(entry.session.id, record, entry.approvalResponder ?? null, agentInfoFromSession(entry))`.
  (When `attachApprovalResponder` is called later, that hook also tells the
  registry; see below.)
- `attachApprovalResponder(entry, runtime)` — additionally call
  `approvalRegistry.attachResponder(entry.session.id, entry.approvalResponder!)`
  so the registry sees newly bound runtime.
- Refactor `performApprovalAction(entry, approvalId, action)` core logic
  into a registry-keyed function `performApprovalActionForEntry(entry,
  record, action)` that the registry's `applyApprovalAction` also calls.
  This keeps a single code path; the existing terminal-scoped route still
  calls `performApprovalAction(entry, ...)`.

## Step 3 — session cleanup helper

**New: `src/lib/terminal-session-cleanup.ts`**

```
export function cleanupTerminalSessionResources(
  id: string, reason: string,
): void {
  approvalRegistry.detachSession(id, reason);
  getTerminalSessions().delete(id);
}
```

Replace each of the 6 `sessions.delete(id)` call sites with this helper:

- `src/lib/terminal-manager.ts:464` (invalid-cwd cleanup)
- `src/lib/terminal-manager-initial-finish.ts:81` (normal finish)
- `src/lib/terminal-manager-initial-child-helpers.ts:374` (early failure)
- `src/lib/orchestration-session-lifecycle.ts:88`
- `src/lib/local-worker.ts:73, 77`
- `src/lib/terminal-test-fixture.ts:81` (test fixture — ensures hermetic
  tests exercise detach too)

After detach, the registry still returns the record on
`GET /api/approvals`; only `actionable` flips to false.

## Step 4 — new route handlers

**New: `src/app/api/approvals/route.ts`** — `GET`

Query params:
- `_repo` (filter by `repoPath`)
- `status` (repeatable / CSV)
- `active` (`true` → only active statuses)
- `updatedSince` (epoch-ms cursor; deterministic ordering)

Response: `{ data: ApprovalEscalation[] }`. Wraps with `withServerTiming`.
On no filter, returns all.

**New: `src/app/api/approvals/[approvalId]/actions/route.ts`** — `POST`

Body: `{ action: "approve" | "always_approve" | "reject" }`. Validates
with `isApprovalAction`. Calls
`approvalRegistry.applyApprovalAction(approvalId, action)`:
- 404 on unknown id
- 200 `{ data: { approvalId, action, status, record } }` on success
- 409 `{ error, code: "approval_responder_unavailable", record }` when
  responder gone — record stays in queue. (We choose 409 over 410 since
  the resource still exists; it just isn't programmatically actionable.
  Acceptance criterion #5 explicitly allows either.)
- 502 on responder failure (preserves existing behaviour from
  `performApprovalAction`'s `reply_failed`).

**Modify: `src/app/api/terminal/[sessionId]/approvals/[approvalId]/route.ts`**

Change `getSession(sessionId)`-based path: still verify the session exists
(maintain 404 on bogus session id for compat), but route the action through
`approvalRegistry.applyApprovalAction` so behaviour matches the canonical
endpoint exactly. Existing UI/clients keep working.

## Step 5 — UI hydration

**New: `src/lib/approval-escalation-api.ts`**

```
export async function fetchApprovalEscalations(
  filter?: { active?: boolean; repoPath?: string; updatedSince?: number },
): Promise<ApprovalEscalation[]>
```

**New: `src/lib/approval-escalation-hydrator.ts`** (or extend the existing
`session-approval-hydration.ts` with a parallel function)

```
export async function hydrateApprovalEscalationsFromApi(): Promise<void> {
  const items = await fetchApprovalEscalations({ active: true });
  for (const item of items) enqueueApprovalEscalation(item, "approval.hydrated");
}
```

**Modify: `src/hooks/use-terminal-panel-effects.ts`**

Replace `hydrateApprovalEscalationsFromSessions(sessions)` (line 29) with
`hydrateApprovalEscalationsFromApi()`. This makes the UI Approvals tab,
bell badge, banner, and toast all observe the same canonical queue that a
direct API-polling agent sees.

`hydrateApprovalEscalationsFromSessions` stays exported for any external
caller, but is no longer the primary path. (Keep one backward-compat fall
back: if the API call fails for any reason, log and let SSE banner upserts
continue.)

## Step 6 — OpenAPI

**New: `src/lib/openapi/schemas-approvals.ts`**

```
ApprovalAction:        enum
ApprovalEscalationStatus: enum
ApprovalReplyTarget:   { adapter, transport, nativeSessionId?, requestId?, permissionId? }
ApprovalAgentInfo:     { provider?, name?, model?, version? }
ApprovalEscalation:    full DTO — approvalId, notificationKey, status,
                       createdAt, updatedAt, repoPath, beatId,
                       terminalSessionId, agent, adapter, source, toolName,
                       serverName, message, question, parameterSummary,
                       toolParamsDisplay, toolUseId, nativeSessionId,
                       requestId, permissionId, permissionName, patterns,
                       options, replyTarget, supportedActions, actionable,
                       actionableReason
ApprovalActionRequest: { action: ApprovalAction }
ApprovalActionResponse: { approvalId, action, status, record: ApprovalEscalation }
ApprovalActionErrorResponse: { error, code, record? }
```

**New: `src/lib/openapi/paths-approvals.ts`**

- `GET /api/approvals` with parameters: `_repo`, `status[]`, `active`,
  `updatedSince`. 200 schema = `{ data: ApprovalEscalation[] }`.
- `POST /api/approvals/{approvalId}/actions` — request/response/409/502/404
- `POST /api/terminal/{sessionId}/approvals/{approvalId}` — documented as
  a compatibility endpoint; same body/response.

**Modify: `src/lib/openapi/schemas.ts`**
- Add `pendingApprovals: { type: "array", items: { $ref: ".../ApprovalEscalation" } }` to `TerminalSession`.

**Modify: `src/lib/openapi-spec.ts`**
- Add the `Approvals` tag.
- Spread `approvalsPaths` into `paths`.

## Step 7 — tests (hermetic, no real processes)

All under `src/**/__tests__/`, inline mocks only — keeps with project policy.

1. **`src/lib/__tests__/approval-registry.test.ts`** *(new)*
   - register a record without any browser/UI; list it via registry; assert
     full DTO shape (approvalId, status, createdAt, updatedAt, repoPath,
     beatId, terminalSessionId, agent fields, adapter/source/tool fields,
     normalized supportedActions, replyTarget, actionable=true).
     *(Acceptance #1.)*
   - filters: `repoPath`, `status`, `activeOnly`, `updatedSince`. Verify
     ordering is `updatedAt desc, approvalId asc`. *(Acceptance #2.)*
   - `detachSession` keeps record visible with `actionable=false` and
     `actionableReason="approval_responder_unavailable"`. *(Acceptance #3.)*
   - `applyApprovalAction` happy path delegates to responder and updates
     status to approved/always_approved/rejected. *(Acceptance #4.)*
   - `applyApprovalAction` after detach returns deterministic 409 +
     `code: approval_responder_unavailable`; record still listed; status
     transitions to `manual_required` (or stays `pending` — TBD; pick
     `manual_required` for clarity since the user must now act manually
     via terminal/CLI). *(Acceptance #5.)*

2. **`src/app/api/approvals/__tests__/route.test.ts`** *(new)*
   - `GET /api/approvals` returns `{ data: [...] }` with full
     ApprovalEscalation shape; respects filters; deterministic order.
   - hermetic: stub `approvalRegistry` directly via vi.mock, or seed
     globalThis registry singleton in `beforeEach`.

3. **`src/app/api/approvals/[approvalId]/actions/__tests__/route.test.ts`** *(new)*
   - POST with each of `approve`, `always_approve`, `reject` → 200, status
     updated, record echoed back. *(Acceptance #4.)*
   - POST after `detachSession` → 409 with deterministic body; record
     remains via subsequent GET. *(Acceptance #5.)*
   - POST with invalid action → 400.
   - POST with unknown approvalId → 404.

4. **`src/lib/__tests__/terminal-approval-session.test.ts`** *(extend)*
   - new test: after `recordPendingApproval`, a separate
     `approvalRegistry.listApprovals()` includes the same record.
   - new test: after calling `cleanupTerminalSessionResources(sessionId)`,
     entry is gone but `approvalRegistry.getApproval(id)` still resolves
     and reports `actionable=false`. *(Acceptance #3.)*

5. **`src/app/api/terminal/[sessionId]/approvals/[approvalId]/__tests__/route.test.ts`** *(new)*
   - exercises the compat route end-to-end: returns same payload shape as
     the canonical route. *(Acceptance #6.)*

6. **`src/lib/openapi/__tests__/paths-approvals.test.ts`** *(new)*
   - import `openApiSpec`; assert presence of `/api/approvals`,
     `/api/approvals/{approvalId}/actions`,
     `/api/terminal/{sessionId}/approvals/{approvalId}`,
     `ApprovalEscalation` schema, and that `TerminalSession` schema lists
     `pendingApprovals`. *(Acceptance #8.)*

7. **`src/hooks/__tests__/use-terminal-panel-effects.test.ts`** *(extend)*
   - replace mock to assert `hydrateApprovalEscalationsFromApi` is called
     instead of (or in addition to) the legacy session-based hydrator.
     *(Acceptance #7.)*

8. **Manual verification (per knot verification step #7)**: run `bun-dev`,
   inject a fixture pending approval through the registry's exported
   helper, curl `/api/approvals?_repo=/Users/cartine/foolery`, then close
   the terminal and re-curl. Documented as a manual test note in the
   handoff capsule, not a hermetic test.

## Step 8 — quality gates

`bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run test`, `bun run build` —
each must pass before exit. Per CLAUDE.md, fix all pre-existing issues
encountered along the way.

## File length watch

`approval-registry.ts` will be ~250 lines (filter logic + DTO mapping).
`paths-approvals.ts` will be ~200 lines (verbose OpenAPI). Both under the
500-line cap. No function should exceed 100 lines (filter pipeline split
into helpers).

## Complexity & risk

**Complexity: medium.** Mostly additive, with one cross-cutting refactor
(centralizing `sessions.delete` through a cleanup helper). No new
runtime dependencies. About 6 new files, ~10 edits.

**Risks:**
1. *Multiple `sessions.delete` call sites already exist.* Missing one
   site means an approval continues to look actionable after the session
   is gone. Mitigation: replace all 6 call sites in one pass; grep
   `sessions.delete\b` is the audit. Add a hermetic test that exercises
   the helper.
2. *Concurrent writes to a record from registry vs entry.* The records
   are shared refs and JS is single-threaded; no lock needed.
3. *UI regression — existing terminal-session pendingApprovals field.*
   The TerminalSession `pendingApprovals` field is preserved for now
   (compat). UI hydration stops depending on it but SSE and direct GET
   /api/terminal users still see it.
4. *Status semantics for detached approvals.* I'm planning to set status
   to `manual_required` on detach so the badge is honest. Mark this
   explicitly in the handoff capsule so the implementer doesn't second-guess
   it.
5. *`actionableReason` taxonomy.* Keep it tied to the existing
   `unsupportedReason` strings (`approval_responder_unavailable`,
   `approval_reply_target_missing`, `approval_action_not_supported`) so
   logs/banners stay consistent with `terminal-approval-session.ts`'s
   existing taxonomy.

## Invariants

The knot has no invariants attached. The repo-level constraints we must
respect (CLAUDE.md):
- "Fail loudly, never silently" — registry MUST throw a
  `FOOLERY DISPATCH FAILURE`-style banner if a configured approval
  resource is missing? **No** — approval handling already has a graceful
  per-record `reply_failed` / `unsupported` taxonomy; that's not a
  silent fallback on configured infra, it's a per-request resolution.
  Keep status taxonomy; no banner needed here.
- Hermetic tests: all new tests use the existing `makeEntry()` pattern,
  vi.fn() responders, and EventEmitter mocks. No `process.env`, fs,
  network, or process spawning.
- File/function/line length: enforced — split helpers as noted.
- No prefix-based state classification: not applicable here (we're
  inside the approval taxonomy, not workflow state).

## Test strategy summary

| Acceptance | Coverage |
|---|---|
| #1 GET /api/approvals shape | registry test + route test |
| #2 Filters & ordering | registry test + route test |
| #3 Records survive cleanup | extended terminal-approval-session test + registry detach test |
| #4 POST happy path | actions route test + registry test |
| #5 Detached responder → 409 | actions route test + registry test |
| #6 Compat route | terminal/[sessionId]/approvals route test |
| #7 UI hydrates from API | use-terminal-panel-effects test |
| #8 OpenAPI coverage | paths-approvals snapshot/shape test |
| #9 Aggregate | all of above |
| #10 Quality gates | lint/tsc/test/build |
